ARGUMENTS PRESENTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE MOTION TO COMPEL IS MOOT, BUT AWARDS SANCTIONS OF $10,000 AGAINST THE DEFENDANT. THE PREVAILING PARTY (PLAINTIFF) SHALL PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER, AS TO FORM FOR REVIEW, AND SUBMIT IT TO THE COURT WILLIAMCLYNN@GMAIL.COM IN WORD FORMAT. JUDGE PRO TEM: WILLIAM LYNN; CLERK: R. DISCOVERY 302, MOTION TO ENFORCE COURT SANCTIONS ORDER AND FOR ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: CONTEMPT AND SANCTIONS FOR VIOLATION OF A COURT ORDER. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER FINDING DEFENDANT JIN IN CONTEMPT OF COURT FOR FAILING TO TIMELY PAY THE $10,000 IN SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS AWARDED AN ADDITIONAL $15,000 IN ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS FOR THE EFFORTS MADE TO SECURE COMPLIANCE WITH THE COURT'S ORDER AND FOR THE BRINGING AND PROSECUTION OF THE PENDING MOTION. [SEE MINI MINUTES FOR FULL DETIALS) AS THE PREVAILING PARTY, COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IS DIRECTED TO PREPARE A CONFORMING AND PROPOSED ORDER IN WORD FORMAT AND SUBMIT IT TO THE COURT AT PSW@HASSARD.COM. FOR REVIEW AND EXECUTION. COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 (APPROVAL AS TO FORM) IS REQUIRED. JUDGE PRO TEM: PHILIP WARD; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/JPT)

PLAINTIFF OPEN FOUNDATION D/B/A OPEN PLATFORM NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO ENFORCE COURT SANCTIONS ORDER AND FOR ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: CONTEMPT AND SANCTIONS FOR VIOLATION OF A COURT ORDER (TENTATIVE RULING PART 2 OF 2)
Off Calendar Mar-22-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Court Sanctions Order And For Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt And Sanctions For Violation Of A Court Order
Off Calendar Mar-22-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF OPEN FOUNDATION D/B/A OPEN PLATFORM'S Motion To Enforce Court Sanctions Order And For Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt And Sanctions For Violation Of A Court Order


The plaintiff is a foreign foundation headquartered in Singapore. The defendant is alleged to be a resident of San Francisco. The plaintiff alleges that the defendant engaged in tortious activity that caused it damage and harm.

In previous proceedings, on October 2, 2023 the Court granted plaintiff's motion to compel the production of documents and awarded sanctions of $10,000 in connection therewith. The order provided that the sanctions were to be paid within thirty days of the service of a notice of entry of order. Those sanctions were thus due pursuant to the order on or before November 2, 2023. Plaintiff initially filed a motion to enforce the award of sanctions which, it claimed, had never been paid. Plaintiff also seeks additional sanctions for bringing the motion to enforce and for related sums authorized under the Code of Civil Procedure.

Shortly after the filing of plaintiff's motion, defendant paid the $10,000 in sanctions previously ordered by the Court. That payment was made well after payment was required under the order. Although defendant at various times claimed financial inability to pay the sanctions, he never provided any evidence in support of that claim either to plaintiff's counsel or the Court. Then, once the instant motion was filed, defendant suddenly became financially able to comply with the October, 2023 order.

For the reasons stated above, plaintiff's motion for an order finding defendant Jin in contempt of court for failing to timely pay the $10,000 in sanctions is GRANTED. Plaintiff is awarded an additional $15,000 in attorney's fees and costs for the efforts made to secure compliance with the Court's order and for the bringing and prosecution of the pending motion. As the prevailing party, counsel for plaintiff is directed to prepare a conforming and proposed order in Word format and submit it to the Court at psw@hassard.com. for review and execution. Compliance with CRC 3.1312 (Approval as to Form) is required.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
